scope: Electric signs, outline lighting systems, and neon tubing installations
adoption_note: Adopted by reference in most US jurisdictions; verify local AHJ amendment cycle (many jurisdictions on 2020 or 2017 NEC).

sections.title: Scope
requirements: Covers electric signs, outline lighting, neon tubing and associated equipment.
Applies to equipment rated 600 V or less.
Excludes signs exempted by 600.2 (portable, battery-powered, etc.).

checklist.item: Confirm installation is an electric sign or outline lighting system as defined in 600.2.
checklist.notes: If portable battery-powered sign: Article 600 may not apply; verify with AHJ.

sections.title: Listing
requirements: All electric signs and outline lighting systems must be Listed and marked for outdoor or indoor use.
Field-assembled signs may use a field evaluation/labeling program if factory listing is not feasible.
Exception: signs rated ≤ 600 V in industrial occupancies may be field-wired without listing if qualified persons maintain them.

checklist.item: Verify sign bears UL Listing mark (or equivalent NRTL mark: ETL, CSA, etc.).
checklist.notes: Accept UL 48, UL 879, UL 879A as applicable listing standards.

checklist.item: If unlisted field-assembled sign: confirm field evaluation label from NRTL is obtained before energizing.
checklist.notes: AHJ may require inspection and field labeling per 600.3 Exception No. 1.

checklist.item: Confirm Indoor/Outdoor rating matches installation location.
checklist.notes: Outdoor-rated sign required for any exposure to rain, weather, or humidity.

sections.title: Markings
requirements: 600.4(A): Nameplate must show manufacturer name or trademark, supply voltage (V), full-load current (A), frequency (Hz), number of signs if multiple on circuit.
600.4(B): Sign must be marked Indoor, Outdoor, or Wet Location as applicable.
600.4(C): Neon tubing sections must be identified to match transformer output rating.
Nameplate must be visible and legible after installation without requiring disassembly.

checklist.item: Nameplate shows manufacturer name/trademark.
Nameplate shows supply voltage rating.
Nameplate shows full-load ampere rating.
Nameplate shows frequency (Hz) for AC supplies.
Sign is marked Indoor / Outdoor / Wet Location per installation type.
Neon sections identified to match transformer/EPS output rating (if neon).

sections.title: Branch Circuits
requirements: 600.5(A): Each commercial building or commercial occupancy accessible to pedestrians must have at least one outlet for a sign or outline lighting system in the entrance/exit area.
600.5(B): Branch circuits supplying signs must be rated 20 A or less.
600.5(C): The branch circuit for a sign must not supply other loads.
600.5(D): The ampacity of conductors must not be less than the load served.

checklist.item: Commercial occupancy with pedestrian access has outlet for sign at entrance (min one outlet per entrance).
Branch circuit is rated 15 A or 20 A (not > 20 A for sign circuits).
checklist.notes: 20 A preferred for LED signs; 15 A acceptable for low-wattage signs.

checklist.item: Sign branch circuit is dedicated — no other loads on the circuit.
Conductor ampacity ≥ sign full-load current (plus derating factors).

sections.title: Disconnecting Means
requirements: 600.6(A)(1): A disconnecting means must be within sight of the sign and within 50 ft of it.
600.6(A)(2): The disconnect must be capable of being locked in the open (off) position.
600.6(A)(3): Only one disconnecting means per sign or outline lighting system.
600.6(B): The disconnect must open all ungrounded conductors simultaneously.
Exception: If the disconnect is in a locked room accessible only to qualified persons, the within-sight requirement is waived.

checklist.item: Disconnect is within sight (visible without obstruction) and within 50 ft of sign.
Disconnect can be locked in open position (hasp, built-in lock, or lockable enclosure).
checklist.notes: NFPA 70E lockout/tagout provisions apply during maintenance.

checklist.item: Disconnect opens all ungrounded conductors simultaneously (single operation).
Only one disconnecting means per sign (not multiple in series).

sections.title: Grounding and Bonding
requirements: 600.7(A): Metal parts of a sign, including enclosures, must be grounded.
600.7(B): Metal parts must be bonded together and to the equipment grounding conductor (EGC).
600.7(C): Bonding conductor must be copper or other corrosion-resistant material sized per Table 250.122.
600.7(D): Bonding jumpers required for metal parts not part of continuous metal raceway.

checklist.item: All exposed metal parts (cabinet, back-pan, channel letters) are grounded.
Metal sign enclosure is bonded to EGC of supply circuit.
checklist.notes: EGC must be sized per NEC Table 250.122 based on OCPD rating.

checklist.item: Bonding conductor is copper, sized ≥ EGC of supply circuit.
Isolated metal sections have individual bonding jumpers to EGC.

sections.title: GFCI Protection
requirements: 600.10(A): Portable cord-connected signs must have GFCI protection for the cord.
600.10(C)(2): Portable signs used in damp or wet locations must have GFCI protection.
600.10(D): Fixed outdoor signs within 6 ft of grade that are accessible to the public must have GFCI protection.
600.10(E): Signs within 6 ft of a pool, spa, fountain, or similar body of water must have GFCI protection.

checklist.item: Cord-connected portable sign: GFCI protection provided (GFCI outlet or GFCI cord set).
Portable sign in damp or wet location: GFCI protection confirmed.
Fixed outdoor sign within 6 ft of finished grade accessible to public: GFCI breaker or GFCI outlet at connection point.
checklist.notes: GFCI at panel (breaker type) or at first outlet serving the sign is acceptable.

checklist.item: Sign within 6 ft of pool/spa/fountain: GFCI protection confirmed.

sections.title: Field-Installed Secondary Wiring
requirements: 600.12(A): Field-installed secondary circuit wiring in a sign must be Listed wiring for the application.
600.12(B): Secondary wiring from neon transformers must be GTO-15 (high-voltage, gas tube) cable rated 15 kV.
600.12(C): LED driver secondary wiring must be rated for the operating temperature and voltage.
Secondary wiring must be protected from physical damage (raceway or conduit if exposed).

checklist.item: All field-installed secondary wiring is Listed for the sign application.
Neon secondary wiring is GTO-15 (15 kV rated) for any runs outside glass tubing.
LED driver secondary wiring rated for operating voltage and min 60°C (preferably 90°C in enclosed cabinets).
Secondary wiring in areas accessible to public is in conduit or otherwise protected.

install_notes: Verify local jurisdiction's NEC adoption year — many use 2017 or 2020 NEC, not 2023.
Pull permit and schedule AHJ inspection before energizing any new sign installation.
Provide as-built drawing showing disconnect location relative to sign for inspector.
NFPA 70E applies to maintenance — use lockout/tagout procedure before servicing.
references: NFPA 70E Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace 2024
International Sign Association (ISA) NEC 600 Installation Guide
NFPA.org — NEC Article 600 public draft documents
